carrier_description: Ammonia (NH3) is a colorless, pungent gas with a molecular weight of 17.03 g/mol. Standard purity constraints typically require 99.5% minimum purity for industrial applications. It is composed of one nitrogen atom and three hydrogen atoms, and is commonly stored and transported as a liquid under high pressure or low temperature. Infrastructure context requires corrosion-resistant materials due to its reactivity with metals.
Standard electricity transmission with typical voltage levels and purity constraints for grid infrastructure.

Methane (CH4) is a colorless, odorless gas primarily used as a fuel source. Standard purity constraints typically require 95%+ purity for industrial applications. Infrastructure context includes pipeline transportation, cryogenic storage, and combustion systems.
